title: Frasera gypsicola
taxonomy: Frasera gypsicola is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Gentianaceae. This plant is native to the southwestern United States and is a member of the genus Frasera. The genus Frasera is further classified as a part of the order Gentianales. Within this order, Frasera gypsicola is also classified as a part of the class Magnoliopsida. It is further classified as a dicot, belonging to the kingdom Plantae.
uses: Frasera gypsicola is a plant used in traditional medicine. It is said to have anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. However, its medicinal uses are not well-documented in mainstream scientific literature. Further research is needed to confirm its potential health benefits. Traditional healers often use it to treat various ailments, including digestive issues and infections.

intro: Frasera gypsicola, also known as Mountain Sneezeweed, is a perennial plant species native to the mountainous regions of the western United States. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, offering potential health benefits including anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and expectorant effects, which may help alleviate respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs. The bioactive constituents of Frasera gypsicola include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and iridoid glycosides, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Mountain Sneezeweed include teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are often used to support respiratory health and alleviate cold and flu symptoms.
